question:
I'm using the split-pr skill on a Megatron-LM PR. According to the skill: (a) what is the EXACT bash one-liner the skill prescribes for extracting file-scoped changes from the source branch onto a new branch (the form is `git diff ... -- <file paths> | ...`), and (b) when each resulting split PR is pushed, must it go to the user's fork or directly to upstream NVIDIA/Megatron-LM? Reply with exactly these two lines and nothing else. Use plain text — no backticks, no quotes, no markdown formatting:
Extraction command: <full bash command>
Push target: <fork or upstream>
ground_truth:
Extraction command: git diff upstream/main..<source-branch> -- <file paths> | git apply
Push target: fork
expected_behavior: Names git diff upstream/main..<source-branch> -- <file paths> | git apply as the extraction one-liner (uses git diff + git apply, not cherry-pick or checkout)
Names the user's fork as the push target (not upstream NVIDIA/Megatron-LM directly)
